name: Sealwater Conclave
identity: Deliberative meeting quarter where confederation decisions are translated into practical obligations.
approach: Visitors meet courtyards, seal rooms, and controlled waiting spaces built to make impatience costly.
inside: Inside, scribes, heralds, and negotiators turn disagreement into managed sequence.
who_is_out_of_place: outsiders treating the confederation as unified and simple
seasonal_change: Traffic and internal competition rise with shipping and formal conclave cycles.
law_context: Umbrella principality customs matter, but house hierarchy and negotiated precedence matter more.
scene_openers: Sealwater Conclave makes confederated power feel negotiated rather than unified.
Within minutes, outsiders learn that precedence is being performed as much as enforced.
social_misstep_examples: acting as though one house speaks for all
outsider_advantage: Fresh entrants notice who is deferring more than they should.
outsider_liability: Wrong assumptions about unity create enemies fast.

arrival_variants_by_time.night: quieter, more broker-driven, and more dangerous to misread

recent_local_change_signal: Veterans insist the houses remain aligned, but no one says it with confidence.
